LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS EIZABETH SHWIFF AND SHWILL, LEVY & POLO, LLP'S MOTION FOR DISCRETIONARY DISCOVERY - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSES SHE IS DISQUALIFIED FROM HEARING ANY MATTERS PERTAINING TO THIS CASE PURSUANT TO CCP 170.1(A)(3)(B)(II) BECAUSE SHE IS A "FIDUCIARY WHO HAS A FINANCIAL INTEREST" IN A PARTY TO THE PROCEEDING. JUDGE MILLER IS TRUSTEE OF A TRUST THAT OWNS CORPORATE BONDS IN DEFENDANT HARTFORD FINANCIAL SERVICES GROUP WITH A FAIR MARKET VALUE IN EXCESS OF $1500. THESE BONDS PAY AN ESTIMATED ANNUAL INCOME OF ABOUT $4800 FOR JUDGE MILLER'S FATHER. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY IS ALSO A DEFENDANT. JUDGE MILLER DOES NOT KNOW ITS RELATIONSHIP TO HARTFORD FINANCIAL SERVICES GROUP. IN ANY EVENT, JUDGE MILLER IS DISQUALIFIED FROM HEARING THIS MATTER ABSENT WAIVER. THE PARTIES MAY CHOOSE TO WAIVE THIS DISQUALIFICATION PURSUANT TO CCP 170.3(B)(1) IN WRITING SIGNED BY ALL PARTIES AND COUNSEL. PARTIES MAY HAVE UNTIL OCTOBER 6, 2014 TO PROVIDE WRITTEN WAIVER TO COURT, OR MAY NOTIFY CLERK SOONER IF ONE OR MORE DO NOT WISH TO WAIVE DISQUALIFICATION (COURTESY COPY TO BE PROVIDED TO DEPT 302). IF NO WAIVER, MATTER WILL BE REASSIGNED TO ANOTHER JUDGE BY PRESIDING JUDGE. IF WAIVER, THE MATTER WILL BE SET ON JUDGE MILLER'S CALENDAR. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MAXIMILLIAN A. CONTRERAS, CSR # 13876 (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS TRESSLER LLP AND PAUL WHITE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO 12/23/14 PER STIPULATION OF COUNSEL. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

Plaintiffs Eizabeth Shwiff And Shwiff, Levy & Polo, Llp'S Motion For Discretionary Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN F. HELFAND & ATTY. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN, TEL. NO. 397-0007
APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. DAVID IAN DALBY OF HINSHAW & CULBERTSON LLP, TEL. NO. 362-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
MAXIMILLIAN A. CONTRERAS OF ATKINSON BAKER, TEL. NO. 421-3021, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - PLAINTIFFS EIZABETH SHWIFF AND SHWILL, LEVY & POLO, LLP'S Motion For Discretionary Discovery - Judge Miller discloses she is disqualified from hearing any matters pertaining to this case pursuant to CCP 170.1(a)(3)(B)(ii) because she is a "fiduciary who has a financial interest" in a party to the proceeding.
Judge is trustee of a trust that owns corporate bonds in Defendant Hartford Financial Services Group with a fair market value in excess of $1500. These bonds pay an estimated annual income of about $4800 for Judge's father. Hartford Casualty Insurance Company is also a defendant. Judge does not know its relationship to Hartford Financial Services Group. In any event, Judge is disqualified from hearing this matter absent waiver. The parties may choose to waive this disqualification pursuant to CCP 170.3(b)(1) in writing signed by all parties and counsel. Parties may have until October 6, 2014 to provide written waiver to Court, or may notify clerk sooner if one or more do not wish to waive disqualification (courtesy copy to be provided to Dept 302). If no waiver, matter will be reassigned to another judge by Presiding Judge. If waiver, the matter will be set on Judge Miller's calendar. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maximillian A. Contreras, CSR # 13876 =302/mjm
